sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.22 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$(G)$be the augmentation ideal of the integer group ring of an arbitrary group$G$. Then$D_n(G)=G (1 + ^n(G))$contains the$n$th lower central subgroup$_n(G)$of$G$. [r]{a)} Is it true that$D_n(G)/ _n(G)$is central in$G/ _n(G)$?
